quote: Perfect. Well, let me go ahead and get this, opened up on the map here so I can take a look at it. While I'm doing that, what's mainly got you guys interested in solar?
stewart_read: Gabe asks the emotional anchor question at the right beat and multitasks it with the map pull — efficient pacing. The question never gets answered because Daniel's response reveals the duplicate, but the setup was correct.
coaching_implication: No coaching needed on the question itself. Use this moment to show the rep they were on the right track before the situation changed — reinforces the correct sequencing habit.

quote: Matter of fact, I was getting ready to send him a copy of my bill.
stewart_read: Daniel volunteering that he was about to send his bill is a strong engagement signal — he was actively cooperating with Ion's process. This is not a coaching failure by Gabe (the call was already in resolution mode), but it is worth surfacing for the manager: this lead is warm and mid-process. The handoff note to Jake should reflect this.
coaching_implication: Flag for the manager to ensure Jake's CRM record captures Daniel's bill-ready status. The lead is warm — Jake should not re-open qualification from scratch. This is a handoff quality issue, not a Gabe coaching issue.

quote: Matter of fact, I was getting ready to send him a copy of my bill.
stewart_read: The ~28-second gap between 01:20 and 01:48 — after Daniel's last statement and before Gabe re-establishes contact — is the call's only execution gap. Gabe did attempt partial bridges at 01:09 ('And Jake shots you a call?'), 01:12, and 01:15, but those were before Daniel's final statement. After 01:20, there is no verbal bridge for approximately 28 seconds. For a customer who was cooperative and waiting, this is a manageable but real friction point. The reactive read is that Gabe simply went silent while working the system. The strategic read — that Gabe was deliberately quiet to avoid interrupting his own system search — is plausible but doesn't require 28 seconds of silence to execute. Reactive read holds on the narrower gap.
alternative_hypothesis: Strategic read: Gabe stayed quiet to focus on the system lookup without creating confusion. Rejected because a brief verbal placeholder ('Still here, just pulling this up') costs nothing and reduces customer uncertainty.
coaching_implication: Coach Gabe on the verbal placeholder habit during system holds: a single line like 'Still here, just pulling this up for you' every 10-15 seconds keeps the customer anchored. This is a low-stakes, high-frequency skill that pays off on longer holds in future calls.

quote: Looks like there was, kind of a duplicate here in our system. And so I'll go ahead and get this clear. And, yeah, just keep working with Jake. He he's got you set up, so I'll go ahead and get this clear.
stewart_read: Gabe's resolution language is functionally correct — he names the issue, defers to Jake, and exits cleanly. The phrase 'kind of a duplicate' is slightly hedged ('kind of') which could introduce mild confusion about what happened, but Daniel's 'Okay' response shows it landed without friction. No schema section currently covers duplicate-lead routing protocol — this is a schema gap. The manager should note that Gabe handled this correctly by instinct, but there is no documented Ion procedure for this scenario.
coaching_implication: Flag to Kenny: duplicate-lead intercept protocol is not documented in the schema. Gabe's handling here is a reasonable baseline for what the procedure should look like — acknowledge the duplicate, defer to the existing rep by name, apologize briefly, exit. Consider formalizing this as a protocol entry so all setters handle it consistently rather than improvising.

stewart_read: Gabe correctly captures that the wife is on the title at 00:31-00:36. Under normal call conditions, this would require scheduling the appointment with both decision-makers present per the spouse protocol. The call ended before this could be actioned, but the capture itself was correct and timely.
coaching_implication: No coaching needed for Gabe on this specific call — the duplicate ended the call before spouse scheduling was relevant. Surface this for Jake: the wife is on the title and must be included in the appointment per protocols.spouse_decision. This is a handoff data point, not a Gabe gap.
